subject: Java 11 migration: fixing problems on 'catalog-fe'
commit_message:
Java 11 migration: fixing problems on 'catalog-fe'

- Upgrade `jersey-bom` version variable to the most recent stable one
- Removed `jersey-common` version variable and replaced its references with `jersey-bom`

Issue-ID: SDC-3036
Signed-off-by: Chris Andre <chris.andre@yoppworks.com>
Change-Id: I414e3814d6d4fc1d927b302efb5dd08659676dc2

> 
> Sounds like a good idea: however, let's not use milestone releases in our dependencies ;-)

somebody said to use 'milestone releases'? 
you just need to replace 
<jersey-common.version>2.22.2</jersey-common.version>
with
<jersey-common.version>${jersey-bom.version}</jersey-common.version>
in 
openecomp-be/api/openecomp-sdc-rest-webapp/application-config-rest/application-config-rest-services/pom.xml

I believe M1 in the version number stands for "Milestone 1"... I was merely pointing to this fact.
Again, I don't have any problems with this version upgrade, just have some reserves about using "3.0.0-M1"
